sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 50-year-old male, presents with a 5-day history of right knee pain.
He reports that the pain started after a skiing accident where he twisted his knee and landed awkwardly, causing pain and swelling.
He denies hearing any popping or feeling any instability in the knee at the time of the injury.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension for which he takes lisinopril 20mg daily.
He denies any previous surgeries except for a tonsillectomy.
He is a frequent water skier and enjoys outdoor activities.
Review of systems: The patient denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77, which is within normal limits.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema on the right knee, with an effusion present.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, negative varus and valgus test, negative Lachman sign, palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, and no lower extremity edema.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain; Hypertension; [NOT MENTIONED]; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily to help reduce swelling and pain.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check for any potential issues related to the patient's hypertension medication.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance purposes.
Follow-up: I will contact the patient to discuss the results of the x-ray and physical therapy plan.
